interpretation: Empirical aP susceptibility-reduction anchor; tightened SD (0.05) to prevent multimodality from beta_S/reporting_multiplier compensation. The posterior is constrained to [0.30, 0.60] which spans the literature range for population-average aP VE against infection.

Prior centered on 0.25 representing the population-average effect of aP vaccination on onward infectiousness. Tightened SD (0.05) to prevent extreme values. Range [0.12, 0.38] spans from fully waned to recently boosted population-average estimates.
Fixed at prior mean (0.10) during MCMC; not identifiable from aggregate case data.

Relative infectiousness of asymptomatic/subclinical infections compared to symptomatic cases. Tightened SD (0.10) to prevent extreme values while still allowing meaningful exploration. Range [0.20, 0.70] spans from minimal subclinical transmission to near-equal infectiousness.

Prior centered on fitness-neutral (1.0) based on epidemiological evidence that MRBP reached near-fixation in China within 8 years and spread globally without apparent transmission disadvantage. SD of 0.12 allows the posterior to explore modest fitness costs (down to ~0.75) or advantages (up to ~1.25). The previous prior (mean 0.95, SD 0.18) was inconsistent with the observed rapid fixation dynamics.

Prior for maternal antibody protection against infant infection. Centered on 0.55 based on Skoff et al. (2017) VE=77.7% against confirmed pertussis in <2mo infants (which combines infection prevention and disease prevention). The infection-blocking component is estimated at 50-60% (Amirthalingam et al. 2014; Baxter et al. 2017). SD of 0.12 allows exploration of [0.30, 0.80].

Prior for maternal antibody protection against symptomatic disease given infection. High confidence based on consistent estimates of VE against hospitalization (>90%) across US, UK, and Argentina studies (Skoff 2017; Amirthalingam 2014; Romanin 2020). Narrow SD reflects strong evidence consensus.
